data.title: Stock index futures tick higher after rally driven by U.S.-U.K. trade deal
data.description:
Stock index futures inched up a day after Wall Street rallied following the announcement of a trade deal between the U.S. and the United Kingdom, while investors hope for more such deals. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.38%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.87%.

Wall Street on Thursday rose after President Donald Trump exhorted traders to “go out and buy stock now” after announcing a trade deal between the U.S. and the United Kingdom—the first of its kind since Trump's imposition of sweeping reciprocal tariffs in April.

“U.S. consumers are worse off than in January, though better off than a week ago. Some U.K. exporters get better conditions, some U.S. exporters might get better conditions. Yesterday’s Bank of England's rate cut was a more important economic event,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Probably more important for markets than the UK “deal” itself were Trump’s comments on China, saying that he expects “substantive” talks that could then yield tariff cuts, Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

In more tariff news, the Trump administration is reportedly considering a significant reduction in tariffs during weekend talks with China, aiming to ease tensions and mitigate the economic strain both countries are beginning to experience.

The economic calendar is light on Friday, with mainly Federal Reserve speakers lined up. 

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee, New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams, and Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack are all scheduled to speak.

data.title: Earnings snapshot: Enbridge Q1 beats profit estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook

data.description:
• SANUWAVE Health press release (NASDAQ:SNWV): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.66.

data.title: Ubiquiti Non-GAAP EPS of $3.00, revenue of $664.2M
data.description:
• Ubiquiti press release (NYSE:UI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.00.
  • Revenue of $664.2M (+34.7% Y/Y).

  • Revenues for the third quarter of fiscal 2025 were $664.2 million, representing an increase from the prior quarter of 10.7% and an increase from the comparable prior year period of 34.7%.

    The increase in revenues compared to the prior quarter as well as the comparable prior year period was driven by an increase in revenue from our Enterprise Technology platform, partially offset by a decrease in revenue from our Service Provider Technology platform.

data.title: Profound Medical targets 70%-75% growth in 2025 with new TULSA AI module launch
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Profound Medical Corporation (PROF) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Arun Menawat highlighted the CAPTAIN trial results presented at the American Urological Association Meeting, describing them as establishing TULSA's statistical superiority over robotic radical prostatectomy. This trial marks the first randomized controlled trial comparing a new technology to the standard of care in prostate cancer treatment.
  • CFO Rashed Dewan reported Q1 2025 revenue of $2.6 million, an 82% increase year-over-year. Recurring revenue contributed $1.8 million, while $820,000 came from capital equipment sales. Gross margin rose to 71% from 60% the previous year.
  • Dr. Mathieu Burtnyk, COO, announced the upcoming limited release of the TULSA AI volume reduction module for treating BPH in June, with a full release planned for the beginning of Q4 2025. The module is expected to expand TULSA’s addressable market significantly.
  • Tom Tamberrino, Chief Commercial Officer, emphasized the company's new TULSA+ program, which combines TULSA with Siemens’ Magnetom FreeMax MRI in a turnkey solution to accelerate adoption. This program aims to reduce operational costs and increase accessibility for urology practices.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed their guidance of 70%-75% revenue growth for 2025, emphasizing a back-loaded trajectory as the TULSA+ program and AI module gain traction.
  • Arun Menawat noted that the CAPTAIN trial results will support efforts to gain inclusion in cancer society guidelines and broader private insurance coverage.
  • The TULSA AI module for BPH is anticipated to significantly streamline procedures, with expected durations of 60-90 minutes and no overnight stays required.

Financial Results

  • The company reported a net loss of $10.7 million for Q1 2025, equating to ($0.36) per share, compared to a net loss of $6.6 million, or ($0.27) per share, in Q1 2024.
  • Operating expenses increased to $13 million in Q1 2025 from $8.7 million in the prior year, attributed to R&D and SG&A investments.
  • As of March 31, 2025, cash reserves stood at $46.4 million.

Q&A

  • Ben Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets: Inquired about feedback on CAPTAIN data from clinicians. Arun Menawat: Highlighted strong interest from teaching hospitals, with positive feedback surpassing expectations.
  • John McAuley, Stifel: Asked about guidance and commercial placement outlook. Tom Tamberrino: Reiterated confidence in achieving 70%-75% growth, citing a shift to a capital sales model and a growing sales pipeline as factors.
  • Michael Freeman, Raymond James: Questioned Medicare and Medicaid patient engagement following CMS reimbursement. Arun Menawat: Noted a gradual pickup in utilization and emphasized ongoing efforts to streamline insurance approvals.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the impact of CAPTAIN data and adoption of the TULSA AI module. Concerns centered on the timeline for broader insurance reimbursement and pacing of revenue growth.
  • Management maintained a confident and proactive tone, emphasizing TULSA’s clinical and economic advantages while addressing operational progress.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue decreased from $4.2 million in Q4 2024 to $2.6 million in Q1 2025, reflecting a typical seasonal slowdown and a transition to a capital sales model.
  • Gross margin remained stable at 71%, consistent with the prior quarter.
  • Management’s tone shifted to a more forward-looking focus, with increased emphasis on strategic initiatives like the TULSA+ program and BPH module launch.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified the need to secure broader private insurance coverage as a key challenge. Efforts to mitigate this include leveraging CAPTAIN trial data and engaging with insurance providers.
  • Analysts raised questions about the timing of revenue realization from new programs and modules, given back-end loading of the 2025 guidance.

Final Takeaway

Profound Medical Corporation is targeting significant growth in 2025, driven by the launch of the TULSA AI module for BPH and the TULSA+ program. The CAPTAIN trial results are poised to enhance adoption and insurance coverage. While challenges like broader reimbursement and revenue pacing remain, management’s focus on clinical validation and strategic expansion underscores their confidence in achieving long-term growth targets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Inspirato targets $235M-$255M revenue for 2025 with focus on profitability and operational efficiency
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Inspirato (ISPO) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Payam Zamani highlighted Q1 2025 as a transformative period for Inspirato, emphasizing the company’s focus on operational efficiencies, brand elevation, member experience, and advancing its technology and digital marketing platform. The company delivered its strongest adjusted EBITDA performance of $5.6 million, attributed to cost discipline and portfolio optimization.
  • Zamani detailed strategic initiatives, including enhancing service quality through updated concierge training and new service standards, and optimizing the non-lease cost of revenue. He also emphasized continued investment in technology to build a scalable digital marketing platform.
  • CFO Michael Arthur reported that operating expenses decreased by $8 million year-over-year, with a similar decline in cost of revenue. Subscription revenue and travel revenue were $21 million and $42 million, respectively.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed full-year 2025 guidance, projecting adjusted EBITDA between breakeven and $5 million and revenue between $235 million and $255 million. Operating expenses are expected to be between $80 million and $90 million, reflecting a 15% reduction year-over-year.
  • The company expects revenue headwinds to persist due to prior reductions in Pass subscriptions but anticipates stabilization in membership trends by the second half of 2025.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 total revenue was approximately $66 million, down 18% year-over-year, driven by a planned decline in Pass subscriptions. Active Club and Pass memberships stood at approximately 10,200 and 1,300, respectively.
  • Free cash flow was negative $8 million, including $2.6 million in one-time expenses related to lease terminations. Adjusted free cash flow, excluding one-time items, was negative $4.5 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $5.6 million marked a new record for the company.

Q&A

  • Logan Hennen, Northland Capital Markets, asked about updates on marketing efforts. CEO Zamani explained that the sales force has been expanded significantly and emphasized the development of a robust technology platform to scale access to luxury travelers.
  • Hennen inquired about the decline in club memberships. CFO Arthur stated that membership headwinds are expected to stabilize in the latter half of 2025, with a focus on acquiring members aligned with the brand.
  • Questions about expense cutting were addressed by Zamani, who highlighted ongoing efforts for operational efficiency, supported by the appointment of a Chief Transformation Officer.
  • On major milestones for 2025, Zamani emphasized achieving sustained profitability, operational efficiency, and launching a scalable digital marketing platform.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concern about declining memberships and revenue headwinds but noted optimism around operational improvements and cost control measures.
  • Management’s tone was confident and forward-looking, emphasizing strategic progress and long-term growth potential. CEO Zamani’s statements reflected a clear focus on achieving profitability and scaling digital marketing capabilities.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management demonstrated increased confidence in achieving operational efficiencies and improving profitability metrics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 remained consistent, with an emphasis on achieving profitability and reducing operating expenses. The tone of the management was more optimistic compared to Q4 2024, reflecting progress in operational efficiency and EBITDA improvement.
  • Membership figures showed a further decrease, but management reiterated a strategy to stabilize and grow memberships by targeting quality acquisitions.
  • Strategic focus shifted towards leveraging technology for digital marketing and member acquisition compared to prior quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Declining revenue and membership numbers continue to pose challenges, with management highlighting expected headwinds throughout 2025.
  • Dependency on cost optimization measures for profitability remains a critical risk.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the timing and effectiveness of the company’s efforts to stabilize membership trends and drive sustainable growth.

Final Takeaway

Inspirato delivered record adjusted EBITDA in Q1 2025, highlighting operational efficiencies and cost discipline as key drivers. While revenue and memberships declined due to strategic shifts, management remains focused on stabilizing trends by the second half of 2025. The reaffirmation of full-year guidance and investments in technology and marketing platforms underscore the company’s forward-looking strategy to achieve sustained profitability and growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wells Fargo issues a warning on containerboard stocks
data.description:
Wells Fargo warned on Friday that it has become incrementally more negative on containerboard stocks due to the expectation for lower demand to crack pricing by the middle part of 2025.

The firm downgraded International Paper Company (NYSE:IP) to Underweight from Equal Weight and lowered its price target on IP to $40 from $45. Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) was cut to an Equal Weight Rating from Overweight and assigned a lowered price target of $180. EPS estimates on Equal Weight-rated Smurfit Westrock Plc (NYSE:SW) were lowered for 2025-2026.

"Though most cboard producers started the year aggressively pushing price, only $40/ton made it through. This increase was already driven by supply-side cost factors & capacity reduction in the face of still tepid demand," updated analyst Gabe Hajde. He noted domestic demand is declining and export markets are slowing orders from the U.S., which is leading to elevated inventories. Hadje and his team believe containerboard producers will no longer be able to defy gravity.

International Paper Company (NYSE:IP) fell 1.9% in premarket trading, while Packaging Corporation of America (NYSE:PKG) was down 1.3%.

data.title: Sylvamo reports mixed Q1 results; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• Sylvamo press release (NYSE:SLVM): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.68 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $821M (-9.3% Y/Y) beats by $5.09M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $90 million (11% margin) vs. $157 million (16% margin).
  • Cash provided by operating activities of $23 million vs. $164 million.
  • Free cash flow of $(25) million vs. $100 million.

  • Second Quarter Outlook

    • Adjusted EBITDA of $75 million to $95 million
    • Compared to the first quarter:
      • Price and mix are expected to improve by $5 million to $10 million due to favorable mix in Latin America and North America
      • Volume is projected to remain stable in the range of $(5) million to $5 million
      • Operations and other costs are expected to improve by $10 million to $15 million primarily due to improving manufacturing operations and seasonally lower operating costs in Europe and North America
      • Input and transportation costs are projected to improve by $5 million to $10 million due to energy
      • Total planned maintenance outage expenses are expected to increase by $36 million
    • We expect quarterly earnings to significantly improve in the second half of the year as we benefit from lower planned maintenance outage expenses, improved commercial results and better operations.

data.title: Federal Agricultural Mortgage Non-GAAP EPS of $4.19 beats by $0.13
data.description:
• Federal Agricultural Mortgage press release (NYSE:AGM): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $4.19 beats by $0.13.
